content.text:
As a senior software engineer you are given wiht the following requiremnts to implement

Build a feature that exports all tasks for a project to a real Airtable base — at the end of the export, open your Airtable base and the tasks must be visible there. Use the official `airtable` npm package for the API calls.
Must work:
A trigger from the project detail page that initiates the export
Only project members (admin or member) can trigger the export
 A server-side endpoint that fetches all tasks and pushes them to Airtable using real API calls
The export must handle being run more than once gracefully
Handle Airtable client errors gracefully: retry transient failures, do not retry permanent failures, do not fail the entire export if a single record fails
Notes:
the system needs to be scalable for large nof of task exports

for the above requirment draft an plan

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
{
  "name": "taskboard",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev --turbopack -p 3000",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start -p 3000",
    "lint": "next lint",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "db:generate": "prisma generate",
    "db:migrate": "prisma migrate deploy",
    "db:migrate:dev": "prisma migrate dev",
    "db:seed": "tsx prisma/seed.ts",
    "db:reset": "prisma migrate reset --force --skip-seed && npm run db:seed",
    "setup": "prisma migrate deploy && prisma generate && npm run db:seed"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/client": "6.1.0",
    "@tanstack/react-query": "5.62.7",
    "airtable": "0.12.2",
    "bcryptjs": "2.4.3",
    "clsx": "2.1.1",
    "jsonwebtoken": "9.0.2",
    "next": "15.5.15",
    "react": "19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "19.0.0",
    "zod": "3.24.1"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@testing-library/jest-dom": "6.6.3",
    "@testing-library/react": "16.1.0",
    "@testing-library/user-event": "14.5.2",
    "@types/bcryptjs": "2.4.6",
    "@types/jsonwebtoken": "9.0.7",
    "@types/node": "22.10.2",
    "@types/react": "19.0.2",
    "@types/react-dom": "19.0.2",
    "autoprefixer": "10.4.20",
    "eslint": "9.17.0",
    "eslint-config-next": "15.5.15",
    "jsdom": "25.0.1",
    "postcss": "8.4.49",
    "prisma": "6.1.0",
    "tailwindcss": "3.4.17",
    "tsx": "4.19.2",
    "typescript": "5.7.2",
    "vitest": "2.1.8"
  }
}
---
app
components
lib
schemas
tests
types

content.content:
1	generator client {
2	  provider = "prisma-client-js"
3	}
4	
5	datasource db {
6	  provider = "postgresql"
7	  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
8	}
9	
10	enum Role {
11	  admin
12	  member
13	  viewer
14	}
15	
16	enum TaskStatus {
17	  todo
18	  in_progress
19	  review
20	  done
21	}
22	
23	model User {
24	  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
25	  email        String
26	  name         String
27	  passwordHash String   @map("password_hash")

file.content:
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

enum Role {
  admin
  member
  viewer
}

enum TaskStatus {
  todo
  in_progress
  review
  done
}

model User {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  email        String
  name         String
  passwordHash String   @map("password_hash")
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt    DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  memberships    Membership[]
  ownedProjects  Project[]    @relation("ProjectOwner")
  assignedTasks  Task[]       @relation("TaskAssignee")
  createdTasks   Task[]       @relation("TaskCreator")
  comments       Comment[]    @relation("CommentAuthor")

  @@map("users")
}

model Project {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  name        String
  description String?
  ownerId     String   @map("owner_id")
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  owner       User         @relation("ProjectOwner", fields: [ownerId], references: [id])
  memberships Membership[]
  tasks       Task[]

  @@map("projects")
}

model Membership {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String   @map("user_id")
  projectId String   @map("project_id")
  role      Role     @default(member)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  user    User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([userId, projectId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("memberships")
}

model Task {
  id          String     @id @default(cuid())
  projectId   String     @map("project_id")
  title       String
  description String?
  status      TaskStatus @default(todo)
  assigneeId  String?    @map("assignee_id")
  createdById String     @map("created_by_id")
  position    Int        @default(0)
  createdAt   DateTime   @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime   @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  project   Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  assignee  User?   @relation("TaskAssignee", fields: [assigneeId], references: [id])
  createdBy User    @relation("TaskCreator", fields: [createdById], references: [id])
  comments  Comment[]

  @@index([projectId, status])
  @@map("tasks")
}

model Comment {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  taskId    String   @map("task_id")
  authorId  String   @map("author_id")
  body      String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  task   Task @relation(fields: [taskId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  author User @relation("CommentAuthor", fields: [authorId], references: [id])

  @@index([taskId, createdAt])
  @@map("comments")
}

content.text:
I have enough context — Next.js 15 App Router, JWT auth with `canEditTasks` (admin|member), `airtable` npm package already in deps, env placeholders for `AIRTABLE_API_KEY` / `AIRTABLE_BASE_ID` / `AIRTABLE_TABLE_NAME`, and a mock client at [src/lib/airtable-mock.ts](src/lib/airtable-mock.ts) for tests. Here is the plan.

---

## Airtable Export — Implementation Plan

### 1. Goal & constraints recap
- Trigger from project detail page → server endpoint → push all tasks of the project to the real Airtable base via the official `airtable` npm package.
- Authorization: admin or member of the project only (viewers blocked).
- **Idempotent**: re-running the export must not duplicate records.
- **Resilient**: retry transient failures, skip permanent ones, never abort the whole export on a single bad record.
- **Scalable**: must handle projects with thousands of tasks without timing out an HTTP request or hitting Airtable's 5 req/sec/base limit.

### 2. Architecture (recommended)

```
[Project page]
    │  POST /api/projects/:id/exports        (start)
    ▼
[Next.js route handler]
    │  authz check → create ExportJob row (status=queued) → enqueue
    ▼
[Background worker]                          ← decoupled from HTTP request
    │  fetch tasks in pages
    │  push to Airtable in batches of 10 via performUpsert
    │  per-record error policy
    │  update ExportJob progress
    ▼
[Airtable base]

[Project page] polls GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId for progress
```

The HTTP endpoint returns **202 Accepted + exportJobId** immediately. UI polls a status endpoint (every ~2s) until `succeeded`/`failed`. This is what makes it scale — a 5,000-task project can't run inline on a Vercel/Next.js request.

For the worker, two options — see decision below.

### 3. Data model changes (Prisma)

Add one model:

```prisma
enum ExportStatus { queued, running, succeeded, failed }

model ExportJob {
  id              String       @id @default(cuid())
  projectId       String       @map("project_id")
  triggeredById   String       @map("triggered_by_id")
  status          ExportStatus @default(queued)
  totalTasks      Int          @default(0) @map("total_tasks")
  successCount    Int          @default(0) @map("success_count")
  failureCount    Int          @default(0) @map("failure_count")
  errors          Json?        // [{ taskId, message, statusCode }]
  startedAt       DateTime?    @map("started_at")
  finishedAt      DateTime?    @map("finished_at")
  createdAt       DateTime     @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  project       Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  triggeredBy   User    @relation(fields: [triggeredById], references: [id])

  @@index([projectId, createdAt])
  @@map("export_jobs")
}
```

No `airtableRecordId` on Task — idempotency is handled by Airtable's `performUpsert` keyed on a `TaskBoardId` field in the Airtable base (see §6). Simpler, no migration on every export.

### 4. API surface

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/projects/:id/exports` | Start export. Authz: `canEditTasks(role)` (admin\|member). Returns `{ exportId, status: "queued" }`, **202**. |
| `GET`  | `/api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId` | Poll status. Returns counts, errors, status. Authz: project membership. |
| `GET`  | `/api/projects/:id/exports` | (optional) list recent exports for history UI. |

The POST handler also: if there is already a `queued` or `running` job for this project, return that one instead of starting a second (single-flight guard — protects Airtable rate limits and keeps idempotency honest).

### 5. Airtable client wrapper — [src/lib/airtable.ts](src/lib/airtable.ts) (new)

Thin module on top of the `airtable` SDK:

```ts
// pseudo-shape
export function getAirtableTable() {
  const apiKey = required('AIRTABLE_API_KEY');
  const baseId = required('AIRTABLE_BASE_ID');
  const table  = required('AIRTABLE_TABLE_NAME');
  return new Airtable({ apiKey }).base(baseId).table(table);
}

export async function upsertTaskBatch(records: TaskRecord[]) {
  // call table.update(records, { performUpsert: { fieldsToMergeOn: ['TaskBoardId'] }, typecast: true })
  // wrapped in retry policy (see §7)
}
```

In tests, this module is mocked to point at `airtable-mock.ts` (existing `vitest` setup).

### 6. Idempotency

- Airtable base must have a **`TaskBoardId`** text field (one-time setup, documented in README).
- Every record we push includes `fields: { TaskBoardId: task.id, Title, Description, Status, Assignee, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt }`.
- We use the SDK's batch update with `performUpsert: { fieldsToMergeOn: ['TaskBoardId'] }` — Airtable matches on `TaskBoardId`, updates if present, inserts if not. Re-running the same export updates in place; **no duplicates**.
- The single-flight job guard (§4) prevents concurrent exports of the same project racing each other.

Fallback if `performUpsert` is unavailable in the SDK version: add `Task.airtableRecordId String?` and do a manual "list existing records by `TaskBoardId` → update vs create" pass. Recommend trying `performUpsert` first.

### 7. Retry & error policy (per-batch)

Classification:

| Airtable error | Class | Action |
|---|---|---|
| 429 (rate limit) | transient | retry with `Retry-After` if present, else exponential backoff |
| 500, 502, 503, 504 | transient | exponential backoff |
| Network / fetch failure | transient | exponential backoff |
| 401, 403 | permanent | abort job, mark `failed` (auth misconfig — no point retrying) |
| 404 (base/table) | permanent | abort job, mark `failed` |
| 422 (invalid field/value) | permanent | record-level: skip that record, continue batch |
| 400 (bad request) | permanent | skip record, continue |

Backoff: `min(2^attempt * 250ms, 8s)` with full jitter, max **5 attempts**.

**"Don't fail the whole export on one record"**: a 422 on a batch of 10 means we retry as 10 individual upserts so the 9 good ones still land. Failed task IDs + reason accumulate into `ExportJob.errors`. Final job status is `succeeded` if `failureCount === 0`, else `succeeded` with errors visible in UI (or `failed` only if a permanent auth/base error aborted the run — your call; I'd surface "partial" as `succeeded` with `failureCount > 0`).

### 8. Scalability

- **Pagination from DB**: `prisma.task.findMany({ where: { projectId }, take: 500, cursor })` — never load everything into memory at once.
- **Batch to Airtable**: 10 records per request (SDK max).
- **Throttle**: token-bucket limiter at 5 req/sec/base (Airtable's hard limit). One worker per base globally.
- **Concurrency cap**: single in-flight batch at a time per job is fine and simplest; only optimize if measured slow.
- **Background execution**: see §9 — this is the key scaling decision.

### 9. Decision needed: how to run the worker

This is the biggest trade-off — I want to confirm before writing code.

questions.question: How should the background export worker run?

options.label: In-process via fireAndForget (Recommended)
options.description: Start an async function from the POST handler, don't await it. Simple, no new infra. Risk: a server restart mid-export leaves a job stuck in 'running' — mitigate with a stale-job sweeper. Fine for the assessment scope; acceptable up to ~10k tasks.

options.label: DB-backed job queue
options.description: Add a tiny polling worker (separate Node process or a `setInterval` inside Next.js) that claims rows from ExportJob with SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED. Survives restarts and crashes. More infra to set up and run.

options.label: External queue (BullMQ + Redis)
options.description: Production-grade: durable, retryable at the queue level, scales horizontally. Requires adding Redis to docker-compose and a worker process. Heaviest lift.

options.label: Synchronous in the request
options.description: Simplest possible — handler awaits the whole export and returns when done. Will time out on Vercel-style hosting for large projects and blocks the user. NOT recommended given the 'scalable for large N' requirement.

questions.question: How should idempotency be keyed in Airtable?

options.label: performUpsert on TaskBoardId field (Recommended)
options.description: Add a `TaskBoardId` text field in the Airtable base. SDK matches & upserts on it. No DB schema change. Documented as a one-time base-setup step.

options.label: Store airtableRecordId on Task
options.description: Add `airtableRecordId String?` to the Task model. On export, update if set else create then save the new id. Adds a migration and a write per new record; resilient if Airtable schema can't be modified.

content.text:
Locked in: **BullMQ + Redis** for the worker, **`performUpsert` on `TaskBoardId`** for idempotency. Here is the rest of the plan with those choices applied.

### 10. UI changes — [src/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx](src/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx) + new component

- Add an **"Export to Airtable"** button next to the project title. Visible only when current user's role is `admin` or `member` (data already in `project.memberships`; cross-reference with `/api/users/me`).
- Click → `POST /api/projects/:id/exports` → store returned `exportId` in component state.
- Render an `<ExportStatus exportId={…} />` panel that polls `GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId` via React Query (`refetchInterval: 2000`, stop when status is terminal). Show `successCount / totalTasks`, spinner, and any per-record errors collapsed.
- Disable the button while a `queued`/`running` job exists for the project.

### 11. Files to add / change

**New**
- [src/lib/airtable.ts](src/lib/airtable.ts) — real Airtable client wrapper, retry policy, batch upsert.
- [src/lib/export-queue.ts](src/lib/export-queue.ts) — BullMQ queue + connection singleton.
- [src/lib/export-worker.ts](src/lib/export-worker.ts) — worker definition: processor function that runs an export job. Started by a separate entrypoint.
- [bin/worker.ts](bin/worker.ts) — `tsx` entrypoint to boot the worker process.
- [src/app/api/projects/[id]/exports/route.ts](src/app/api/projects/[id]/exports/route.ts) — `POST` start, `GET` list.
- [src/app/api/projects/[id]/exports/[exportId]/route.ts](src/app/api/projects/[id]/exports/[exportId]/route.ts) — `GET` status.
- [src/components/ExportToAirtableButton.tsx](src/components/ExportToAirtableButton.tsx) — trigger + status UI.
- [src/schemas/export.ts](src/schemas/export.ts) — Zod schemas for response shapes.
- [prisma/migrations/…/migration.sql](prisma/migrations) — `ExportJob` table.
- [src/tests/api/exports.test.ts](src/tests/api/exports.test.ts), [src/tests/lib/airtable-retry.test.ts](src/tests/lib/airtable-retry.test.ts).

**Modified**
- [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma) — add `ExportJob` + `ExportStatus`, relations on `Project` / `User`.
- [src/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx](src/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx) — mount export button.
- [package.json](package.json) — add `bullmq`, `ioredis`, a `worker` script (`tsx bin/worker.ts`).
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml) — add a `redis:7` service and a `worker` service running the same image with command `npm run worker`.
- [.env.example](.env.example) — add `REDIS_URL`.
- [README.md](README.md) — document `TaskBoardId` field setup in the Airtable base + how to run the worker.

### 12. Test plan

- **Unit (mock client via `airtable-mock.ts`)**
  - Retries `rate-limit`, `server-error`, `network` up to 5 attempts with backoff; gives up after.
  - 401/403/404/422 are not retried.
  - 422 inside a batch → falls back to per-record upserts; one bad row doesn't kill the other 9.
  - Idempotency: running the export twice on the same project ends with `__getRecordCount() === task count` (no duplicates).
- **API**
  - `POST /api/projects/:id/exports` requires auth (`401`), requires membership (`403`), denies `viewer` (`403`), allows `admin`/`member`.
  - Second concurrent POST returns the existing in-flight job, doesn't enqueue a second.
  - `GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId` enforces project membership.
- **Worker** (integration, with mocked SDK)
  - Pages through 5,000 tasks without OOM (use `take: 500`).
  - Throttle: never more than 5 Airtable calls per second observed.
  - Job ends `succeeded` with `failureCount === N` when N records hit permanent errors.

### 13. Open risks / things I'll watch

- **Airtable SDK + `performUpsert`**: I'll verify the installed `airtable@0.12.2` actually supports `performUpsert` on `update()`. If it doesn't, the fallback is the `airtableRecordId`-on-Task approach — same external behaviour, slightly more DB churn.
- **Vercel-style serverless**: BullMQ workers need a long-running process; if the hosting target is purely serverless this needs a separate Render/Fly/Heroku worker dyno. The README will state this assumption.
- **Schema in Airtable**: we don't auto-create the `Tasks` table or its fields. The README will list the required schema (Title text, Description long-text, Status single-select with the four enum values, Assignee text, TaskBoardId text, CreatedAt date, UpdatedAt date). Missing fields will surface as 422s and be reported per-record.
- **Cancellation / cleanup**: out of scope for v1 — happy to add a "cancel export" endpoint later.

---

Want me to proceed with implementation in this order: (1) Prisma migration + worker scaffolding, (2) Airtable wrapper + retry, (3) API routes, (4) UI, (5) tests? Or adjust the plan first?
we need to handle the worker in between failures

Good catch — the §9 BullMQ choice gives us most of this for free, but the plan needs an explicit failure-recovery section. Here it is.

## 14. Worker failure handling (addendum)

### Failure modes we need to survive

| # | Failure | What goes wrong without handling |
|---|---|---|
| F1 | Worker process crashes mid-export (OOM, deploy, SIGKILL) | `ExportJob.status=running` forever; Airtable has partial data; user can't recover without manual cleanup |
| F2 | Worker dies between Airtable batch sent and response received | Records may or may not have landed; counters out of sync with reality |
| F3 | Redis transiently unavailable | Queue calls fail; jobs not enqueued or workers can't pull |
| F4 | Two workers grab the same job (lock race) | Double-counting; possible duplicate writes |
| F5 | Permanent error mid-export (e.g. base wrong key) | Should abort cleanly, not retry forever |
| F6 | Worker takes a lock then hangs (slow Airtable, no crash) | Lock stays held; job stuck, no other worker can take over |

### Schema additions on `ExportJob`

```prisma
attempts             Int        @default(0)
lastProcessedTaskId  String?    @map("last_processed_task_id")   // resume cursor
heartbeatAt          DateTime?  @map("heartbeat_at")              // worker liveness
bullJobId            String?    @map("bull_job_id")               // for ops/inspection
```

### Recovery primitives

**1. Checkpoint after every batch (F1, F2)**
After each successful Airtable batch, the worker writes:
```
UPDATE export_jobs
SET last_processed_task_id = $cursor,
    success_count = success_count + $n_success,
    failure_count = failure_count + $n_failure,
    errors = errors || $new_errors,
    heartbeat_at = NOW()
WHERE id = $jobId
```
On retry the worker reads `lastProcessedTaskId` and resumes the Prisma cursor pagination from there. Pages already processed are not re-queried. The half-sent batch from F2 *is* re-pushed, but `performUpsert` on `TaskBoardId` makes that a no-op — **idempotency is what makes resume safe**.

**2. BullMQ job retries (F1, F3 transient, F6)**
```ts
queue.add('export', { exportJobId }, {
  attempts: 5,
  backoff: { type: 'exponential', delay: 2000 },
  removeOnComplete: 100,
  removeOnFail: 500,
})
```
- If the worker dies, BullMQ's stalled-job detection re-enqueues the job after `stalledInterval` (default 30s).
- If the processor throws, BullMQ retries with exponential backoff up to `attempts`.
- Permanent-error path (§7) throws an `UnrecoverableError` from BullMQ — skips remaining attempts, job goes to `failed` immediately (F5).

**3. Heartbeat (F1, F6)**
Worker calls `job.updateProgress({ processed, total })` AND updates `ExportJob.heartbeatAt` every batch (cheap — once per ~10 records). If `heartbeatAt` is older than 2× expected batch time, the status endpoint surfaces "stalled" in the UI rather than misleading the user with "running".

**4. Lock duration tuned to batch time (F4, F6)**
`lockDuration: 30_000`, `lockRenewTime: 15_000`. As long as a batch finishes inside 30s, the lock renews; if a batch hangs longer than 30s, BullMQ lets another worker pick it up. The cost of duplicate processing is bounded by `performUpsert` idempotency (no Airtable dupes) — the only side effect is that `successCount` may briefly over-count. We compensate at job completion by recomputing counts from `errors.length` + Airtable's response counts, not by trusting the running totals blindly.

**5. Stalled-job sweeper (F1 defense-in-depth)**
A BullMQ repeatable job that runs every 60s:
```
SELECT id FROM export_jobs
WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
  AND heartbeat_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes'
  AND attempts < 5
```
…re-enqueues those into BullMQ. Catches the edge case where BullMQ itself loses the job (Redis flush, queue rename, version upgrade). Belt-and-braces; the primary mechanism is BullMQ's own stalled detection.

**6. Graceful shutdown (F1 prevention)**
```ts
process.on('SIGTERM', async () => {
  await worker.close();          // wait for current batch to finish
  await queue.close();
  process.exit(0);
});
```
BullMQ's `worker.close()` blocks until the in-flight job's current `await` resolves. Combined with checkpointing per batch, a clean SIGTERM (deploy, rolling restart) loses at most an in-flight batch — which the next pickup safely replays.

**7. Atomic claim transition (F4)**
When the worker starts a job:
```sql
UPDATE export_jobs
SET status = 'running', started_at = COALESCE(started_at, NOW()),
    attempts = attempts + 1, heartbeat_at = NOW()
WHERE id = $jobId AND status IN ('queued', 'running')
RETURNING *
```
Conditional update prevents a worker from resurrecting a job another process already marked `succeeded`/`failed`. The `IN ('queued', 'running')` allows resume-after-crash.

**8. Permanent-error abort (F5)**
The retry classifier from §7 throws `UnrecoverableError` for 401 / 403 / 404-on-base. The worker catches it, sets `status='failed'` with the error reason, and BullMQ stops retrying. Per-record permanent errors (422 on one row) are recorded in `errors` and the export continues — only base-wide permanent errors abort.

### Status endpoint reflects all of this

`GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId` returns:
```
{
  status: 'queued' | 'running' | 'stalled' | 'succeeded' | 'failed',
  totalTasks, successCount, failureCount,
  attempts, lastHeartbeatAgoMs,
  errors: [{ taskId, message, statusCode }]
}
```
`stalled` is derived (not stored): `status='running' && now() - heartbeatAt > 2min`. UI shows a warning + a "retry" button that calls `POST /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId/retry` (a thin wrapper that re-enqueues the existing job row rather than creating a new one — preserves the cursor).

### Tests to add for failure handling

- **Resume**: start a job, kill the worker after batch 3, restart — final Airtable record count equals task count, no duplicates, `successCount` equals task count.
- **Stalled detection**: a worker that `await`s forever → BullMQ re-enqueues after `stalledInterval`, the second worker completes the job.
- **Permanent abort**: 401 from Airtable on first call → job ends `failed` after 1 attempt, not 5.
- **Per-record permanent error**: one row 422s → job ends `succeeded` with `failureCount=1`, other 9 in batch land.
- **Concurrent trigger guard**: two POSTs while a job is `running` → both return the same `exportId`, no second BullMQ job enqueued.
- **Graceful shutdown**: SIGTERM during a batch → batch completes, checkpoint written, exit code 0.

---

This adds two real bits of complexity on top of §1-13: the **checkpoint cursor + idempotent replay** (the load-bearing piece), and the **stalled sweeper** (defense in depth). Everything else is BullMQ config.

Right — `refetchInterval: 2000` from K open project tabs × M concurrent exports hits Postgres K×M / 2 times per second, all reading the same row. Redis as the read path. Here's the revised design.

## 15. Redis-cached status (replaces §10's polling design)

### Cache topology

| Key | Value | Writer | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| `export:status:{exportId}` | JSON snapshot of status response | worker (write-through) | 5 min while running, 1 h after terminal |
| `export:active:{projectId}` | `{exportId}` of currently `queued`/`running` job | POST handler on enqueue, worker on terminal (DEL) | none (deleted on completion) |

`export:active:{projectId}` doubles as the **single-flight guard** — POST does `SET NX` to atomically claim "only one active export per project", instead of a DB transaction. Race-free, no `SELECT FOR UPDATE`.

### Status snapshot shape (what the worker writes)

```json
{
  "exportId": "ck…",
  "projectId": "cp…",
  "status": "running",
  "totalTasks": 4823,
  "successCount": 2510,
  "failureCount": 3,
  "attempts": 1,
  "heartbeatAt": "2026-05-13T12:34:56.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-05-13T12:34:57.110Z",
  "errorsTruncated": true,
  "errorsSample": [ /* first 20 only */ ]
}
```

**Errors are capped** in the cache value (first 20 + a `truncated: true` flag). Full errors stay in `ExportJob.errors` (Postgres JSON) and are only fetched when the user expands the "errors" panel — that becomes the one cold-path DB read.

### Write path (worker)

After every batch the worker writes both:
```ts
await tx.exportJob.update({ where: { id }, data: checkpointPatch });   // durable
await redis.set(`export:status:${id}`, JSON.stringify(snapshot),
                'EX', isTerminal(status) ? 3600 : 300);                 // hot read
```
On terminal status the worker also `DEL export:active:{projectId}`.

DB is the source of truth (checkpoint, recovery, history). Redis is a derived read cache — if it goes down, status reads degrade to DB; nothing is lost.

### Read path (status endpoint)

```ts
GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId
  ├─ authz: membership check (DB, cheap PK lookup — see below)
  ├─ GET export:status:{exportId} from Redis  ──► hit: return  (Redis-only path)
  └─ miss → SELECT from export_jobs → SET Redis → return       (cold path)
```
Hit-rate target: ~100% during a live export (worker re-writes every batch, ~once per 2-10s — well inside the 5-min TTL). After terminal, hot for an hour so the UI's tail-off polls don't touch DB.

### What about the membership authz read?

That's the second per-poll cost. Two options:
- **v1 (recommended)**: leave membership as a Prisma `findUnique` on `(userId, projectId)`. It's a single B-tree lookup, ~0.2ms. Most apps live with this.
- **v2 (if profiling shows it matters)**: short-TTL cache `member:{userId}:{projectId} → role` with TTL 60s. Invalidated by membership change writes.

I'd ship v1 and only add v2 if measurement says it's needed.

### Going further: SSE instead of polling (optional v2)

Polling — even cache-backed — still costs N requests/min/client. The clean answer is **push**:

- Worker `PUBLISH export:updates:{exportId} <snapshot>` after each batch.
- New endpoint `GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId/stream` is a Next.js streaming Response that `SUBSCRIBE`s and writes Server-Sent Events.
- UI uses `EventSource` instead of `useQuery` with `refetchInterval`.

Cost: ~0 requests after connection; one Redis subscriber per open tab (Redis handles 10k+ subs easily). Worth it once you have many concurrent exports or always-on dashboards. I'd build the cache layer now and SSE second, since SSE depends on the same Redis snapshot.

### Updated file list (delta from §11)

**New**
- [src/lib/redis.ts](src/lib/redis.ts) — shared `ioredis` client (already pulled in by BullMQ; just expose it).
- [src/lib/export-status-cache.ts](src/lib/export-status-cache.ts) — `getStatus(id)`, `setStatus(id, snapshot)`, `clearActive(projectId)`, `tryClaimActive(projectId, exportId)`.

**Modified**
- [src/lib/export-worker.ts](src/lib/export-worker.ts) — call `setStatus` after every batch and on terminal transitions; `DEL` active key on terminal.
- [src/app/api/projects/[id]/exports/route.ts](src/app/api/projects/[id]/exports/route.ts) — replace DB transaction-based single-flight with `SET NX` on `export:active:{projectId}`.
- [src/app/api/projects/[id]/exports/[exportId]/route.ts](src/app/api/projects/[id]/exports/[exportId]/route.ts) — read Redis first, DB fallback, cache-on-miss.

### Failure semantics (sanity check against §14)

- **Worker crash before cache write** — DB is checkpointed first, then Redis. Cache may be one batch stale but never wrong; recovery is unchanged.
- **Redis down** — POST returns 503 (queue also unavailable). Status reads fall through to DB. Workers fail to enqueue; BullMQ will retry on reconnect. Existing in-flight jobs continue against Postgres-only state.
- **Cache poisoning / stale value after worker resume** — every successful batch overwrites the snapshot, so within one batch the cache self-heals. Stalled detection (heartbeat older than 2 min) is computed at read time from `heartbeatAt` inside the snapshot, not stored as state — so even a stale cache surfaces "stalled" correctly.

### Tests to add

- Status endpoint: 100 sequential reads with a populated cache → 1 DB query total (auth) + 0 `export_jobs` queries.
- Worker writes Redis snapshot whose `successCount` matches DB after each batch.
- Cache miss → DB read → cache backfilled with correct TTL.
- POST single-flight: 50 concurrent POSTs with cold Redis → exactly 1 BullMQ job enqueued, 49 return the same `exportId`.
- Redis flushed mid-export → status endpoint serves stale-then-refreshed correctly; worker continues unaffected.

Good call — user-initiated reads + cache means traffic is bounded by clicks, not timers. The polling/SSE complexity collapses into a simpler two-tier UX. Final §10 + §15 revision:

## 16. Status UX — pull-on-demand (replaces §10's polling, supersedes §15's polling assumptions)

### Two-tier model

**Tier 1 — default: refresh button.**
After `POST /api/projects/:id/exports` returns, the UI shows a static card:

```
Export started 2m ago · 2510 / 4823 tasks · 3 failures
                                            [⟲ Refresh]
```

`Refresh` → one `GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId`. Cache-backed, ~constant latency. No timer, no background polling. User comes back in 10 minutes, clicks once, sees current state.

**Tier 2 — opt-in: "Watch live" toggle.**
A small toggle on the same card. When enabled, opens an SSE connection to `/api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId/stream`. Auto-closes when the user toggles off, navigates away, or the job hits a terminal state. Default off → zero connections unless explicitly requested.

### Why this works at scale

- **No timer-driven traffic.** Server load is proportional to *user attention*, not job count × open tabs.
- **Refresh hits Redis only.** The worker's write-through cache (§15) keeps the snapshot fresh, so every refresh click is a single Redis `GET` + the auth membership check. Postgres `export_jobs` is never read on the hot path.
- **SSE is the escape hatch for power users / dashboards.** Implemented as a Redis pub/sub subscriber, so a worker `PUBLISH` after each batch fans out to every live subscriber for free. If nobody is watching, no subscribers exist and pub/sub is a no-op.
- **Terminal jobs cost nothing.** Cache TTL 1 h after terminal handles "users coming back the same afternoon"; older lookups fall through to DB once, then recache.

### Edge UX

- **Stale indicator.** Card shows `Last refreshed 2m ago` so the user knows they're looking at a snapshot, not live state.
- **Auto-refresh on terminal arrival** (Tier 2 only): SSE will deliver the final snapshot, then the client closes the stream and disables the toggle. Tier 1 stays manual to the end — fine because by the time the user clicks refresh and sees `succeeded`, they're done.
- **Browser notification (optional).** If the user grants permission *and* enabled "Watch live," fire a notification on terminal. Skip for v1.
- **Initial state.** POST handler returns the full first snapshot in its response body, so the card has data without needing a follow-up GET.

### Endpoints (final)

| Method | Path | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/projects/:id/exports` | Auth → `SET NX export:active:{projectId}` → enqueue → return initial snapshot, **202** |
| `GET` | `/api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId` | Auth → Redis-first, DB fallback → return snapshot. **Only called on user click.** |
| `GET` | `/api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId/stream` | Auth → SSE; subscribes to `export:updates:{exportId}` Redis channel; closes on terminal or client disconnect |
| `GET` | `/api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId/errors` | Cold path — full `errors` JSON from DB. Called only when user expands "View all errors". |

### Worker write fan-out (one extra line vs §15)

```ts
const snapshot = buildSnapshot(job);
await redis.set(`export:status:${id}`, JSON.stringify(snapshot),
                'EX', isTerminal(status) ? 3600 : 300);
await redis.publish(`export:updates:${id}`, JSON.stringify(snapshot));
```
Free for non-subscribed channels. Worker doesn't care whether anyone is listening.

### Tests

- Refresh click with cache populated → 0 `export_jobs` queries, 1 Redis `GET`.
- Refresh click with cache cold → 1 DB read, cache backfilled, second click is Redis-only.
- SSE: worker publishes 5 batch updates → subscriber receives 5 events in order; subscriber disconnect releases the Redis subscription.
- "Watch live" toggled off mid-export → connection closes within 100ms; no further events delivered.
- 100 concurrent refresh clicks on a running export → 100 Redis GETs, 0 export_jobs reads, p99 latency stays flat.

---

That's the whole status path: **POST returns initial snapshot · Refresh hits Redis · SSE is opt-in · DB only on cache miss or full-error expansion**. The §14 failure-handling and §15 cache mechanics are unchanged — just the read trigger moved from timer to user.

# Airtable Export — Final Plan

Consolidated from prior iterations. Implementation-ready.

---

## 1. Summary

Add an export feature that pushes all tasks of a project to a real Airtable base, triggered from the project detail page. Built on:

- **BullMQ + Redis** for the background worker (durable queue, automatic retries, stalled-job recovery).
- **`performUpsert` on a `TaskBoardId` field** in the Airtable base for idempotency.
- **Cursor-based checkpointing** for crash-resume, made safe by upsert idempotency.
- **Redis write-through cache** for the status snapshot.
- **Pull-on-demand UX**: refresh button (Redis-backed) by default, opt-in SSE for live updates.
- Per-record error tolerance, classified retry policy, single-flight job guard per project.

---

## 2. Architecture

```
[Project page]
  │ POST /api/projects/:id/exports
  ▼
[Next.js handler] ── authz (admin|member)
  │ SET NX export:active:{projectId}      ← single-flight guard
  │ INSERT export_jobs                    ← durable record
  │ queue.add('export', { exportJobId })  ← BullMQ enqueue
  │ return 202 + initial snapshot
  ▼
[BullMQ Worker — separate Node process: `bin/worker.ts`]
  │ atomic claim: UPDATE export_jobs SET status='running' …
  │ loop: cursor pagination from lastProcessedTaskId
  │   ├─ build batch of 10 tasks
  │   ├─ airtable.update(batch, performUpsert: { fieldsToMergeOn: ['TaskBoardId'] })
  │   ├─ classify errors, retry transient, fallback to per-record on partial 422
  │   ├─ checkpoint to DB (cursor + counts + errors)
  │   ├─ write Redis snapshot + PUBLISH export:updates:{id}
  │   └─ heartbeat
  │ on terminal: DEL export:active:{projectId}
  ▼
[Airtable base]

[Project page] (user-initiated)
  ├─ Refresh click → GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:id (Redis-first, DB fallback)
  └─ "Watch live" toggle → SSE /api/projects/:id/exports/:id/stream  (Redis pub/sub)
```

---

## 3. Data model — [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)

```prisma
enum ExportStatus {
  queued
  running
  succeeded
  failed
}

model ExportJob {
  id                   String       @id @default(cuid())
  projectId            String       @map("project_id")
  triggeredById        String       @map("triggered_by_id")
  status               ExportStatus @default(queued)
  totalTasks           Int          @default(0) @map("total_tasks")
  successCount         Int          @default(0) @map("success_count")
  failureCount         Int          @default(0) @map("failure_count")
  errors               Json?
  attempts             Int          @default(0)
  lastProcessedTaskId  String?      @map("last_processed_task_id")
  heartbeatAt          DateTime?    @map("heartbeat_at")
  bullJobId            String?      @map("bull_job_id")
  startedAt            DateTime?    @map("started_at")
  finishedAt           DateTime?    @map("finished_at")
  createdAt            DateTime     @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  project       Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  triggeredBy   User    @relation(fields: [triggeredById], references: [id])

  @@index([projectId, createdAt])
  @@map("export_jobs")
}
```

Add the inverse relations on `Project` and `User`. No fields added to `Task` — idempotency lives in Airtable.

---

## 4. API surface

| Method | Path | Authz | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/projects/:id/exports` | `admin` \| `member` | `SET NX export:active:{projectId} = exportId`; if already set, return existing job. Else insert row, enqueue BullMQ job, return initial snapshot, **202** |
| `GET` | `/api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId` | project member | Redis-first read of `export:status:{exportId}`; DB fallback, cache backfill |
| `GET` | `/api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId/stream` | project member | SSE; subscribes to Redis `export:updates:{exportId}`; closes on terminal or disconnect |
| `GET` | `/api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId/errors` | project member | Full `errors` JSON from DB (cold path, only on "view all errors" click) |
| `GET` | `/api/projects/:id/exports` | project member | (optional) recent jobs list for history UI |

Authz helpers from [src/lib/auth.ts](src/lib/auth.ts): `getCurrentUser`, `getProjectMembership`, `canEditTasks(role)`.

## 5. Airtable client — [src/lib/airtable.ts](src/lib/airtable.ts)

```ts
// shape only — full implementation in step 3
import Airtable from 'airtable';

export function getAirtableTable() {
  return new Airtable({ apiKey: env.AIRTABLE_API_KEY })
    .base(env.AIRTABLE_BASE_ID)
    .table(env.AIRTABLE_TABLE_NAME);
}

export type TaskFields = {
  TaskBoardId: string;     // upsert key
  Title: string;
  Description: string | null;
  Status: 'todo' | 'in_progress' | 'review' | 'done';
  Assignee: string | null;
  CreatedAt: string;
  UpdatedAt: string;
};

export async function upsertBatch(records: { fields: TaskFields }[]) {
  // wraps table.update(records, {
  //   performUpsert: { fieldsToMergeOn: ['TaskBoardId'] },
  //   typecast: true,
  // }) inside the retry classifier (§7)
}
```

### Retry classifier

| Airtable error | Class | Action |
|---|---|---|
| 429 | transient | retry honoring `Retry-After`, else exp backoff |
| 500 / 502 / 503 / 504 | transient | exp backoff |
| network / fetch failure | transient | exp backoff |
| 401 / 403 | permanent | throw `UnrecoverableError` → job fails |
| 404 (base / table) | permanent | throw `UnrecoverableError` → job fails |
| 422 (invalid field) on batch | partial | retry batch as N individual upserts; failures recorded per-record |
| 400 on single record | permanent (per-record) | record into `errors`, continue |

Backoff: `min(2^attempt * 250ms, 8s)` with full jitter. Max **5 attempts** per call.

### Airtable base setup (one-time, documented in README)

Required `Tasks` table fields:
- `TaskBoardId` — single-line text (upsert key)
- `Title` — single-line text
- `Description` — long text
- `Status` — single-select with options `todo`, `in_progress`, `review`, `done`
- `Assignee` — single-line text
- `CreatedAt`, `UpdatedAt` — date/time

Missing fields surface as per-record 422s and are reported in the UI rather than aborting the export.

---

## 6. Worker — [src/lib/export-worker.ts](src/lib/export-worker.ts) + [bin/worker.ts](bin/worker.ts)

Processor outline:

```ts
async function processExport(job) {
  const { exportJobId } = job.data;

  // 1. atomic claim
  const claimed = await prisma.exportJob.updateMany({
    where: { id: exportJobId, status: { in: ['queued', 'running'] } },
    data: {
      status: 'running',
      startedAt: { /* COALESCE preserved via raw if needed */ },
      attempts: { increment: 1 },
      heartbeatAt: new Date(),
    },
  });
  if (claimed.count === 0) return; // already terminal

  const exportJob = await prisma.exportJob.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: exportJobId } });
  let cursor = exportJob.lastProcessedTaskId ?? undefined;

  // 2. determine total once
  if (exportJob.totalTasks === 0) {
    const total = await prisma.task.count({ where: { projectId: exportJob.projectId } });
    await prisma.exportJob.update({ where: { id: exportJobId }, data: { totalTasks: total } });
  }

  // 3. paginate + batch
  const PAGE = 100;
  while (true) {
    const tasks = await prisma.task.findMany({
      where: { projectId: exportJob.projectId },
      include: { assignee: true },
      orderBy: { id: 'asc' },
      take: PAGE,
      ...(cursor && { skip: 1, cursor: { id: cursor } }),
    });
    if (tasks.length === 0) break;

    for (const batch of chunk(tasks, 10)) {
      const { successCount, failures } = await upsertBatch(batch.map(toFields));
      await checkpoint(exportJobId, batch.at(-1)!.id, successCount, failures);
      await publishSnapshot(exportJobId);
      await job.updateProgress({ processed: ... , total: ... });
    }
    cursor = tasks.at(-1)!.id;
  }

  // 4. terminal
  await prisma.exportJob.update({
    where: { id: exportJobId },
    data: { status: 'succeeded', finishedAt: new Date() },
  });
  await redis.del(`export:active:${exportJob.projectId}`);
  await publishSnapshot(exportJobId);
}
```

BullMQ config:

```ts
new Queue('exports', { connection });
new Worker('exports', processExport, {
  connection,
  concurrency: 2,            // per worker process; scale horizontally beyond that
  lockDuration: 30_000,
  lockRenewTime: 15_000,
  stalledInterval: 30_000,
});

queue.add('export', { exportJobId }, {
  attempts: 5,
  backoff: { type: 'exponential', delay: 2000 },
  removeOnComplete: 100,
  removeOnFail: 500,
});
```

Graceful shutdown:

```ts
process.on('SIGTERM', async () => {
  await worker.close();   // waits for in-flight batch to settle
  await queue.close();
  process.exit(0);
});
```

---

## 7. Failure handling

| Failure | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Worker crash mid-export | Cursor + counts checkpointed per batch; on retry, resume from `lastProcessedTaskId`. Idempotent `performUpsert` makes a replayed batch a no-op. |
| Worker dies between Airtable send and response | Same as above — replay is safe. |
| Two workers grab the same job | BullMQ Redis lock + atomic claim `UPDATE … WHERE status IN ('queued','running')`. Worst case is a brief over-count which Airtable upsert makes harmless. |
| Worker hangs | `lockDuration` 30s with `lockRenewTime` 15s; if no renewal, BullMQ marks stalled and re-enqueues. |
| Permanent error (401/403/404) | Classifier throws `UnrecoverableError` → BullMQ skips remaining attempts → job goes to `failed`. |
| Per-record 422 | Batch falls back to per-record upserts; failures recorded; export continues. |
| Redis down | POST returns 503 (queue unavailable). In-flight workers continue against DB; status reads fall through to DB. Recovery automatic on Redis reconnect. |
| BullMQ loses the job | Stalled-job sweeper (repeatable BullMQ job, every 60s) finds rows with `status IN ('queued','running') AND heartbeat_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '5 min' AND attempts < 5` and re-enqueues. |
| Server restart between POST and enqueue | Either the row exists with no Bull job (sweeper picks it up) or BullMQ already accepted it (durable in Redis). |

---

## 8. Redis cache + SSE

### Keys

| Key | Value | Writer | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| `export:active:{projectId}` | `{exportId}` of in-flight job | POST (`SET NX`); worker on terminal (`DEL`) | none |
| `export:status:{exportId}` | snapshot JSON | worker after every batch and on terminal | 300s running, 3600s terminal |
| `export:updates:{exportId}` (pub/sub channel) | snapshot JSON | worker `PUBLISH` after every batch | n/a |

### Snapshot shape

```json
{
  "exportId": "ck…",
  "projectId": "cp…",
  "status": "running",
  "totalTasks": 4823,
  "successCount": 2510,
  "failureCount": 3,
  "attempts": 1,
  "heartbeatAt": "2026-05-13T12:34:56.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-05-13T12:34:57.110Z",
  "errorsTruncated": true,
  "errorsSample": [ /* first 20 */ ]
}
```

Full errors only via `GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId/errors` (cold DB path).

### Read path

```
GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId
  ├─ authz (membership: DB PK lookup — cheap)
  ├─ GET export:status:{exportId} ──► hit: return
  └─ miss → SELECT export_jobs → SET cache → return
```

### Write path (worker, every batch)

```ts
await prisma.exportJob.update({ where: { id }, data: checkpoint });
const snapshot = buildSnapshot(...);
await redis.set(`export:status:${id}`, JSON.stringify(snapshot),
                'EX', isTerminal(status) ? 3600 : 300);
await redis.publish(`export:updates:${id}`, JSON.stringify(snapshot));
```

`stalled` is a *derived* status — computed at read time from `heartbeatAt` (>2 min old while `status='running'`), never persisted. Self-healing.

## 9. UI — [src/components/ExportToAirtableButton.tsx](src/components/ExportToAirtableButton.tsx)

Mounted on [src/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx](src/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx) next to the project title.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Export to Airtable                          │
```

## 13. Risks / open questions

- **`airtable@0.12.2` and `performUpsert`**: verify the installed version actually supports `performUpsert` on `update()`. If not, fall back to storing `airtableRecordId` on `Task` and doing manual update-vs-create — same external behaviour, one extra DB write per new record.
- **Deployment target for the worker**: BullMQ workers need a long-running Node process. If hosting is purely serverless (Vercel Functions), the worker has to run on a separate platform (Render / Fly / Heroku / Railway worker dyno). README states this assumption.
- **Airtable base schema drift**: we don't auto-create fields. Missing fields → per-record 422s, surfaced in the UI errors panel rather than aborted.
- **Cancellation**: not in v1. Easy follow-up — a `POST …/exports/:id/cancel` that sets a cancel flag the worker checks between batches.
- **Multi-region / multi-base**: out of scope. Single Airtable base, single Redis, single queue.

drop sse

# Airtable Export — Final Plan (SSE removed)

Status updates are pull-only via the Refresh button, backed by the Redis cache. Worker writes the snapshot to Redis after every batch; reads hit Redis with DB fallback. No streaming, no pub/sub, no background polling.

---

## 1. Summary

Add an export feature that pushes all tasks of a project to a real Airtable base, triggered from the project detail page.

- **BullMQ + Redis** for the background worker (durable queue, automatic retries, stalled-job recovery).
- **`performUpsert` on a `TaskBoardId` field** in the Airtable base for idempotency.
- **Cursor-based checkpointing** for crash-resume, made safe by upsert idempotency.
- **Redis write-through cache** for the status snapshot; refresh button reads it.
- Per-record error tolerance, classified retry policy, single-flight job guard per project.

---

## 2. Architecture

```
[Project page]
  │ POST /api/projects/:id/exports
  ▼
[Next.js handler] ── authz (admin|member)
  │ SET NX export:active:{projectId}      ← single-flight guard
  │ INSERT export_jobs                    ← durable record
  │ queue.add('export', { exportJobId })  ← BullMQ enqueue
  │ return 202 + initial snapshot
  ▼
[BullMQ Worker — separate Node process: `bin/worker.ts`]
  │ atomic claim: UPDATE export_jobs SET status='running' …
  │ loop: cursor pagination from lastProcessedTaskId
  │   ├─ build batch of 10 tasks
  │   ├─ airtable.update(batch, performUpsert: { fieldsToMergeOn: ['TaskBoardId'] })
  │   ├─ classify errors, retry transient, fallback to per-record on partial 422
  │   ├─ checkpoint to DB (cursor + counts + errors)
  │   ├─ SET Redis snapshot (write-through cache)
  │   └─ heartbeat
  │ on terminal: DEL export:active:{projectId}
  ▼
[Airtable base]

[Project page] (user-initiated only)
  └─ Refresh click → GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:id (Redis-first, DB fallback)
```

---

## 3. Data model — [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)

```prisma
enum ExportStatus {
  queued
  running
  succeeded
  failed
}

model ExportJob {
  id                   String       @id @default(cuid())
  projectId            String       @map("project_id")
  triggeredById        String       @map("triggered_by_id")
  status               ExportStatus @default(queued)
  totalTasks           Int          @default(0) @map("total_tasks")
  successCount         Int          @default(0) @map("success_count")
  failureCount         Int          @default(0) @map("failure_count")
  errors               Json?
  attempts             Int          @default(0)
  lastProcessedTaskId  String?      @map("last_processed_task_id")
  heartbeatAt          DateTime?    @map("heartbeat_at")
  bullJobId            String?      @map("bull_job_id")
  startedAt            DateTime?    @map("started_at")
  finishedAt           DateTime?    @map("finished_at")
  createdAt            DateTime     @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  project       Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  triggeredBy   User    @relation(fields: [triggeredById], references: [id])

  @@index([projectId, createdAt])
  @@map("export_jobs")
}
```

Add inverse relations on `Project` and `User`. No fields added to `Task` — idempotency lives in Airtable.

## 4. API surface

| Method | Path | Authz | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/projects/:id/exports` | `admin` \| `member` | `SET NX export:active:{projectId} = exportId`; if already set, return existing job. Else insert row, enqueue BullMQ job, return initial snapshot, **202** |
| `GET` | `/api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId` | project member | Redis-first read of `export:status:{exportId}`; DB fallback, cache backfill |
| `GET` | `/api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId/errors` | project member | Full `errors` JSON from DB (cold path, only on "view all errors" click) |
| `GET` | `/api/projects/:id/exports` | project member | (optional) recent jobs list for history UI |

Authz helpers from [src/lib/auth.ts](src/lib/auth.ts): `getCurrentUser`, `getProjectMembership`, `canEditTasks(role)`.

## 5. Airtable client — [src/lib/airtable.ts](src/lib/airtable.ts)

```ts
import Airtable from 'airtable';

export function getAirtableTable() {
  return new Airtable({ apiKey: env.AIRTABLE_API_KEY })
    .base(env.AIRTABLE_BASE_ID)
    .table(env.AIRTABLE_TABLE_NAME);
}

export type TaskFields = {
  TaskBoardId: string;     // upsert key
  Title: string;
  Description: string | null;
  Status: 'todo' | 'in_progress' | 'review' | 'done';
  Assignee: string | null;
  CreatedAt: string;
  UpdatedAt: string;
};

export async function upsertBatch(records: { fields: TaskFields }[]) {
  // wraps table.update(records, {
  //   performUpsert: { fieldsToMergeOn: ['TaskBoardId'] },
  //   typecast: true,
  // }) inside the retry classifier (§7)
}
```

### Retry classifier

| Airtable error | Class | Action |
|---|---|---|
| 429 | transient | retry honoring `Retry-After`, else exp backoff |
| 500 / 502 / 503 / 504 | transient | exp backoff |
| network / fetch failure | transient | exp backoff |
| 401 / 403 | permanent | throw `UnrecoverableError` → job fails |
| 404 (base / table) | permanent | throw `UnrecoverableError` → job fails |
| 422 (invalid field) on batch | partial | retry batch as N individual upserts; failures recorded per-record |
| 400 on single record | permanent (per-record) | record into `errors`, continue |

Backoff: `min(2^attempt * 250ms, 8s)` with full jitter. Max **5 attempts** per call.

### Airtable base setup (one-time, documented in README)

Required `Tasks` table fields:
- `TaskBoardId` — single-line text (upsert key)
- `Title` — single-line text
- `Description` — long text
- `Status` — single-select with options `todo`, `in_progress`, `review`, `done`
- `Assignee` — single-line text
- `CreatedAt`, `UpdatedAt` — date/time

Missing fields surface as per-record 422s and are reported in the UI rather than aborting the export.

---

## 6. Worker — [src/lib/export-worker.ts](src/lib/export-worker.ts) + [bin/worker.ts](bin/worker.ts)

```ts
async function processExport(job) {
  const { exportJobId } = job.data;

  // 1. atomic claim
  const claimed = await prisma.exportJob.updateMany({
    where: { id: exportJobId, status: { in: ['queued', 'running'] } },
    data: { status: 'running', attempts: { increment: 1 }, heartbeatAt: new Date() },
  });
  if (claimed.count === 0) return; // already terminal

  const exportJob = await prisma.exportJob.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: exportJobId } });
  let cursor = exportJob.lastProcessedTaskId ?? undefined;

  // 2. determine total once
  if (exportJob.totalTasks === 0) {
    const total = await prisma.task.count({ where: { projectId: exportJob.projectId } });
    await prisma.exportJob.update({ where: { id: exportJobId }, data: { totalTasks: total } });
  }

  // 3. paginate + batch
  const PAGE = 100;
  while (true) {
    const tasks = await prisma.task.findMany({
      where: { projectId: exportJob.projectId },
      include: { assignee: true },
      orderBy: { id: 'asc' },
      take: PAGE,
      ...(cursor && { skip: 1, cursor: { id: cursor } }),
    });
    if (tasks.length === 0) break;

    for (const batch of chunk(tasks, 10)) {
      const { successCount, failures } = await upsertBatch(batch.map(toFields));
      await checkpoint(exportJobId, batch.at(-1)!.id, successCount, failures);
      await writeSnapshot(exportJobId);   // write-through to Redis
      await job.updateProgress({ processed, total });
    }
    cursor = tasks.at(-1)!.id;
  }

  // 4. terminal
  await prisma.exportJob.update({
    where: { id: exportJobId },
    data: { status: 'succeeded', finishedAt: new Date() },
  });
  await redis.del(`export:active:${exportJob.projectId}`);
  await writeSnapshot(exportJobId);
}
```

BullMQ config:

```ts
new Queue('exports', { connection });
new Worker('exports', processExport, {
  connection,
  concurrency: 2,            // per worker process; scale horizontally beyond that
  lockDuration: 30_000,
  lockRenewTime: 15_000,
  stalledInterval: 30_000,
});

queue.add('export', { exportJobId }, {
  attempts: 5,
  backoff: { type: 'exponential', delay: 2000 },
  removeOnComplete: 100,
  removeOnFail: 500,
});
```

Graceful shutdown:

```ts
process.on('SIGTERM', async () => {
  await worker.close();   // waits for in-flight batch to settle
  await queue.close();
  process.exit(0);
});
```

---

## 7. Failure handling

| Failure | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Worker crash mid-export | Cursor + counts checkpointed per batch; on retry, resume from `lastProcessedTaskId`. Idempotent `performUpsert` makes a replayed batch a no-op. |
| Worker dies between Airtable send and response | Same as above — replay is safe. |
| Two workers grab the same job | BullMQ Redis lock + atomic claim `UPDATE … WHERE status IN ('queued','running')`. Worst case is a brief over-count which Airtable upsert makes harmless. |
| Worker hangs | `lockDuration` 30s with `lockRenewTime` 15s; if no renewal, BullMQ marks stalled and re-enqueues. |
| Permanent error (401/403/404) | Classifier throws `UnrecoverableError` → BullMQ skips remaining attempts → job goes to `failed`. |
| Per-record 422 | Batch falls back to per-record upserts; failures recorded; export continues. |
| Redis down | POST returns 503 (queue unavailable). In-flight workers continue against DB; status reads fall through to DB. Recovery automatic on Redis reconnect. |
| BullMQ loses the job | Stalled-job sweeper (repeatable BullMQ job, every 60s) finds rows with `status IN ('queued','running') AND heartbeat_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '5 min' AND attempts < 5` and re-enqueues. |
| Server restart between POST and enqueue | Either the row exists with no Bull job (sweeper picks it up) or BullMQ already accepted it (durable in Redis). |

---

## 8. Redis cache

### Keys

| Key | Value | Writer | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| `export:active:{projectId}` | `{exportId}` of in-flight job | POST (`SET NX`); worker on terminal (`DEL`) | none |
| `export:status:{exportId}` | snapshot JSON | worker after every batch and on terminal | 300s running, 3600s terminal |

### Snapshot shape

```json
{
  "exportId": "ck…",
  "projectId": "cp…",
  "status": "running",
  "totalTasks": 4823,
  "successCount": 2510,
  "failureCount": 3,
  "attempts": 1,
  "heartbeatAt": "2026-05-13T12:34:56.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-05-13T12:34:57.110Z",
  "errorsTruncated": true,
  "errorsSample": [ /* first 20 */ ]
}
```

Full errors only via `GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId/errors` (cold DB path).

### Read path

```
GET /api/projects/:id/exports/:exportId
  ├─ authz (membership: DB PK lookup — cheap)
  ├─ GET export:status:{exportId} ──► hit: return
  └─ miss → SELECT export_jobs → SET cache → return
```

### Write path (worker, every batch)

```ts
await prisma.exportJob.update({ where: { id }, data: checkpoint });
const snapshot = buildSnapshot(...);
await redis.set(`export:status:${id}`, JSON.stringify(snapshot),
                'EX', isTerminal(status) ? 3600 : 300);
```

`stalled` is a *derived* status — computed at read time from `heartbeatAt` (>2 min old while `status='running'`), never persisted. Self-healing.

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

enum Role {
  admin
  member
  viewer
}

enum TaskStatus {
  todo
  in_progress
  review
  done
}

model User {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  email        String
  name         String
  passwordHash String   @map("password_hash")
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt    DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  memberships    Membership[]
  ownedProjects  Project[]    @relation("ProjectOwner")
  assignedTasks  Task[]       @relation("TaskAssignee")
  createdTasks   Task[]       @relation("TaskCreator")
  comments       Comment[]    @relation("CommentAuthor")

  @@map("users")
}

model Project {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  name        String
  description String?
  ownerId     String   @map("owner_id")
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  owner       User         @relation("ProjectOwner", fields: [ownerId], references: [id])
  memberships Membership[]
  tasks       Task[]

  @@map("projects")
}

model Membership {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String   @map("user_id")
  projectId String   @map("project_id")
  role      Role     @default(member)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  user    User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([userId, projectId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("memberships")
}

model Task {
  id          String     @id @default(cuid())
  projectId   String     @map("project_id")
  title       String
  description String?
  status      TaskStatus @default(todo)
  assigneeId  String?    @map("assignee_id")
  createdById String     @map("created_by_id")
  position    Int        @default(0)
  createdAt   DateTime   @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime   @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  project   Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  assignee  User?   @relation("TaskAssignee", fields: [assigneeId], references: [id])
  createdBy User    @relation("TaskCreator", fields: [createdById], references: [id])
  comments  Comment[]

  @@index([projectId, status])
  @@map("tasks")
}

model Comment {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  taskId    String   @map("task_id")
  authorId  String   @map("author_id")
  body      String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  task   Task @relation(fields: [taskId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  author User @relation("CommentAuthor", fields: [authorId], references: [id])

  @@index([taskId, createdAt])
  @@map("comments")
}

generator client {

}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

enum Role {
  admin
  member
  viewer
}

enum TaskStatus {
  todo
  in_progress
  review
  done
}

model User {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  email        String
  name         String
  passwordHash String   @map("password_hash")
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt    DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  memberships    Membership[]
  ownedProjects  Project[]    @relation("ProjectOwner")
  assignedTasks  Task[]       @relation("TaskAssignee")
  createdTasks   Task[]       @relation("TaskCreator")
  comments       Comment[]    @relation("CommentAuthor")

  @@map("users")
}

model Project {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  name        String
  description String?
  ownerId     String   @map("owner_id")
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  owner       User         @relation("ProjectOwner", fields: [ownerId], references: [id])
  memberships Membership[]
  tasks       Task[]

  @@map("projects")
}

model Membership {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String   @map("user_id")
  projectId String   @map("project_id")
  role      Role     @default(member)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  user    User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([userId, projectId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("memberships")
}

model Task {
  id          String     @id @default(cuid())

  assignee  User?   @relation("TaskAssignee", fields: [assigneeId], references: [id])
  createdBy User    @relation("TaskCreator", fields: [createdById], references: [id])
  comments  Comment[]

  @@index([projectId, status])
  @@map("tasks")
}

model Comment {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  taskId    String   @map("task_id")
  authorId  String   @map("author_id")
  body      String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  task   Task @relation(fields: [taskId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  author User @relation("CommentAuthor", fields: [authorId], references: [id])

  @@index([taskId, createdAt])
  @@map("comments")
}

  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

enum Role {
  admin
  member
  viewer
}

enum TaskStatus {
  todo
  in_progress
  review
  done
}

enum ExportStatus {
  queued
  running
  succeeded
  failed
}

model User {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  email        String
  name         String
  passwordHash String   @map("password_hash")
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt    DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  memberships    Membership[]
  ownedProjects  Project[]    @relation("ProjectOwner")
  assignedTasks  Task[]       @relation("TaskAssignee")
  createdTasks   Task[]       @relation("TaskCreator")
  comments       Comment[]    @relation("CommentAuthor")
  exportJobs     ExportJob[]  @relation("ExportTriggeredBy")

  @@map("users")
}

model Project {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  name        String
  description String?
  ownerId     String   @map("owner_id")
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  owner       User         @relation("ProjectOwner", fields: [ownerId], references: [id])
  memberships Membership[]
  tasks       Task[]
  exportJobs  ExportJob[]

  @@map("projects")
}

model Membership {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String   @map("user_id")
  projectId String   @map("project_id")
  role      Role     @default(member)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  user    User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([userId, projectId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("memberships")
}

model Task {
  id          String     @id @default(cuid())
  projectId   String     @map("project_id")
  title       String
  description String?
  status      TaskStatus @default(todo)
  assigneeId  String?    @map("assignee_id")
  createdById String     @map("created_by_id")
  position    Int        @default(0)
  createdAt   DateTime   @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt   DateTime   @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  project   Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  assignee  User?   @relation("TaskAssignee", fields: [assigneeId], references: [id])
  createdBy User    @relation("TaskCreator", fields: [createdById], references: [id])
  comments  Comment[]

  @@index([projectId, status])
  @@map("tasks")
}

model Comment {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  taskId    String   @map("task_id")
  authorId  String   @map("author_id")
  body      String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  task   Task @relation(fields: [taskId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  author User @relation("CommentAuthor", fields: [authorId], references: [id])

  @@index([taskId, createdAt])
  @@map("comments")
}

services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: taskboard
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: taskboard
      POSTGRES_DB: taskboard
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  web:
    build: .
    command: npm run dev
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - node_modules:/app/node_modules
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      - db
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://taskboard:taskboard@db:5432/taskboard?schema=public
      JWT_SECRET: dev-secret-change-me
      NODE_ENV: development

  test:
    build: .
    command: npm test
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - node_modules:/app/node_modules
    depends_on:
      - db
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://taskboard:taskboard@db:5432/taskboard_test?schema=public
      JWT_SECRET: test-secret
      NODE_ENV: test

volumes:
  pgdata:
  node_modules:

    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: taskboard

      AIRTABLE_API_KEY: ${AIRTABLE_API_KEY:-}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: taskboard
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: taskboard
      POSTGRES_DB: taskboard
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    volumes:
      - redisdata:/data

  web:
    build: .
    command: npm run dev
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - node_modules:/app/node_modules
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      - db
      - redis
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://taskboard:taskboard@db:5432/taskboard?schema=public
      JWT_SECRET: dev-secret-change-me
      NODE_ENV: development
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      AIRTABLE_API_KEY: ${AIRTABLE_API_KEY:-}
      AIRTABLE_BASE_ID: ${AIRTABLE_BASE_ID:-}
      AIRTABLE_TABLE_NAME: ${AIRTABLE_TABLE_NAME:-Tasks}

  worker:
    build: .
    command: npm run worker
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - node_modules:/app/node_modules
    depends_on:
      - db
      - redis
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://taskboard:taskboard@db:5432/taskboard?schema=public
      NODE_ENV: development
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      AIRTABLE_API_KEY: ${AIRTABLE_API_KEY:-}
      AIRTABLE_BASE_ID: ${AIRTABLE_BASE_ID:-}
      AIRTABLE_TABLE_NAME: ${AIRTABLE_TABLE_NAME:-Tasks}

  test:
    build: .
    command: npm test
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - node_modules:/app/node_modules
    depends_on:
      - db
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://taskboard:taskboard@db:5432/taskboard_test?schema=public
      JWT_SECRET: test-secret
      NODE_ENV: test

volumes:
  pgdata:
  node_modules:

# TaskBoard — Project Management App

A Next.js 15 fullstack application for managing projects, tasks, and team members. TypeScript + Prisma + PostgreSQL on the server, React 19 + TanStack Query on the client.

## Quick Setup (Docker — Recommended)

```bash
# Clone and enter the repo
git clone <repo-url> && cd taskboard

# Start the app and database
docker-compose up --build

# In a separate terminal, set up the database
docker-compose exec web npm run db:seed

# Run the test suite
docker-compose exec web npm test

# The app is now running at http://localhost:3000
```

## Manual Setup (without Docker)

Requires: Node.js 20+, PostgreSQL 15+

```bash
# Run the setup script (installs deps, sets up DB, configures git hooks)
chmod +x bin/setup
./bin/setup

# Or do it manually:
npm install
git config core.hooksPath .git-hooks
cp .env.example .env   # then edit DATABASE_URL if your local Postgres differs
npx prisma migrate deploy
npx prisma generate
npm run db:seed
npm test
npm run dev
```

## AI Tool Conversation Tracking

**This repository is configured to automatically capture your AI coding tool conversation history with each git commit.** This includes conversations from Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, Continue.dev, Cody, Cline, and Windsurf.

This is part of the Ajackus evaluation process. We evaluate how you collaborate with AI tools — your prompting strategy, how you break down problems, and how you review AI suggestions. The captured conversations help us understand your workflow.

**How it works:**
- A pre-commit git hook runs automatically before each commit
- It copies conversation files from AI tool directories (e.g., `.claude/`, `.cursor/`) into `.ai-conversations/`
- These files are staged and included in your commit
- You don't need to do anything — it happens automatically

**What's captured:** Only AI tool conversation logs stored in the project directory. No system files, browsing history, or anything outside this repository.

**If you prefer a tool that doesn't store local conversations** (like browser-based ChatGPT), the screen recording will capture your interactions instead. No additional action needed from you.

## Seed Data

The seed file creates:
- 5 users across 3 projects with different roles (admin / member / viewer)
- 3 projects with realistic task distributions
- 12 tasks spanning all four statuses (`todo`, `in_progress`, `review`, `done`)

All user passwords are: `password123`

| Email | Role on which project |
|-------|----------------------|
| meera@taskboard.dev | admin on Q3 Launch & Internal Tools, member on Onboarding |
| arjun@taskboard.dev | admin on Onboarding, member on Q3 Launch |
| kavya@example.com | member on Q3 Launch |
| dev@example.com | viewer on Q3 Launch |
| lina@example.com | member on Onboarding |

## Authentication

Register or login to get a JWT token:

```bash
# Login
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"meera@taskboard.dev","password":"password123"}'

# Use the returned token
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://localhost:3000/api/projects
```

## API Endpoints

### Auth
- `POST /api/auth/register` — Create account
- `POST /api/auth/login` — Sign in, get JWT
- `GET /api/users/me` — Current user (authenticated)

### Projects
- `GET /api/projects` — List projects you're a member of (authenticated)
- `POST /api/projects` — Create a project (authenticated; creator becomes admin)
- `GET /api/projects/:id` — Project detail with tasks and members (authenticated)
- `PATCH /api/projects/:id` — Update project (authenticated)
- `DELETE /api/projects/:id` — Delete project (authenticated)

### Tasks
- `GET /api/projects/:id/tasks` — List tasks in a project (authenticated)
- `POST /api/projects/:id/tasks` — Create a task (authenticated)
- `PATCH /api/tasks/:id` — Update a task (authenticated)
- `DELETE /api/tasks/:id` — Delete a task (authenticated)

## Tech Stack

- Node.js 20 (runtime)
- Next.js 15 (App Router) / React 19
- TypeScript 5 (strict mode)
- Prisma 6 + PostgreSQL 16
- TanStack Query 5 (client data)
- Zod 3 (schema validation)
- Tailwind CSS 3
- bcryptjs + jsonwebtoken
- Vitest 2 (testing)

- `DELETE /api/tasks/:id` — Delete a task (authenticated)
